ground_truth.title: Nutrition Reviews
ground_truth.main_content: Nutrition Reviews is a leading peer-reviewed journal dedicated to the publication of authoritative literature reviews that analyze and synthesize existing and emerging knowledge in the field of nutrition science. It is published monthly by the International Life Sciences Institute (ILSI) in partnership with Oxford University Press. Editor-in-Chief: Douglas Taren, PhD, MS. Associate Editors include Brian Finck, PhD (Washington University in St. Louis), Corrine Hanson, PhD, RD, LMNT, FAND (University of Nebraska Medical Center), Kathleen Holton, PhD (American University), Joshua W. Miller, PhD (Rutgers University), and others. Managing Editor: James M. Cameron, MA. Journal Impact Factor 2024: 5.9, Five-Year Impact Factor: 6.6. The journal is ranked 13th among nutrition and dietetics journals. Each month, Nutrition Reviews publishes a new podcast episode featuring conversations with authors of reviews published in the journal. The journal curates collections of articles on timely topics including the most highly cited articles, articles on omega-3 fatty acids, nutrition and cognitive function, and reviews on stress, sleep, and nutrition. Nutrition Reviews was founded in 1942 in response to a recognized need for expert analysis and synthesis of nutrition science research. The journal welcomes submissions on current and emerging topics in nutrition science, food science, clinical nutrition, and related disciplines including microbiology, biochemistry, public health, psychology, sociology, and economics. Contact: nutritionreviews@ilsi.org
